channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Brits have enough unused cables to go to the moon and back
item.description: There are 627 million unused cables in Britain and £266 million worth of copper hidden away in usused devices.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Drasi: A lightweight approach to event-driven programming
item.description: The Microsoft Azure Incubations team is one of the more interesting components of Microsoft’s hyperscale cloud. It is something between a traditional software development team and a research organization working to build solutions to the problems of distributed systems at massive scale.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What to expect at GITEX 2024: Key technologies shaping the future for tech leaders
item.description: As one of the largest and most influential technology exhibitions in the world, GITEX Global 2024 promises to be a pivotal event for technology leaders.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI as a catalyst for ESG: Empowering CIOs to drive sustainable innovation
item.description: In today’s fast-evolving business landscape, environmental, social and governance (ESG) criteria have become fundamental to corporate responsibility and long-term success.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: OpenAI says cybercriminals are using ChatGPT more to influence elections
item.description: OpenAI has seen a number of attempts where its AI models have been used to generate fake content, including long-form articles and social media comments, aimed at influencing elections, the ChatGPT maker said in a report on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: IT Unemployment Rate Drops by 37%: A Shift in the Job Market?
item.description: The IT unemployment rate has dropped by nearly 37%, marking a significant shift in the job market. Several factors, including a strengthening economy and increased hiring by small-to-midsize companies, are contributing to this trend.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: BMC breaks into two: Mainframe and Helix businesses to operate independently
item.description: BMC today announced plans to divide its existing company into two standalone software companies, one focusing on its long-standing mainframe business and the other focusing on tools for managing IT and distributed operations. Houston-based BMC has been owned by global investment firm KRR since 2018.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI Advancements and Challenges: Experts Weigh In
item.description: Artificial intelligence developments, Nobel Prize winners, EV transition assistance, and safety concerns discussed by experts
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft brings Copilot AI tools to OneDrive
item.description: Microsoft’s Copilot is now available in OneDrive, part of a wider revamp of the company’s cloud storage platform. Microsoft highlighted several ways to use the generative AI (genAI) assistant in OneDrive in a blog post Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google scientists share Nobel Prize in chemistry for AI protein work
item.description: Two Google DeepMind scientists shared the 2024 Nobel Prize in chemistry with a US professor for their breakthrough research into proteins.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Energy leader Petrobras sails toward sustainability with new supply chain solution
item.description: Brazilian oil, natural gas, and energy corporation Petrobras’ longtime mission has been to be the best in its field in terms of value creation with an emphasis on environmental responsibility.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Linus Torvalds annoyed by passive language in commit messages
item.description: The latest release candidate of Linux 6.12 was announced over the weekend, promoting some comments about grammar from Linux founder Linus Torvalds.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service. How it works
item.description: With Hurricane Milton barreling toward Florida's Gulf Coast, many Tampa Bay area residents are fearing widespread and potentially weeks-long power outages, which could affect cellphone service, as many saw during Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, The Hill

item.title: Someone reinvented the toilet so that you can do your business anywhere
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how the Generation 2 Reinvented Toilet transforms waste into safe materials, a promising sanitation solution that could transform lives and communities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WebSockets under the hood with Node.js
item.description: WebSockets is a network communication protocol that enables two-way client-server communication. WebSockets are often used for applications that require instantaneous updates, using a persistent duplex channel atop HTTP to support real-time interactions without constant connection negotiation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service
item.description: The new update allows users to connect their phones to satellite, as long its an iPhone 14 or later model running iOS18.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Former Google engineer and ‘Godfather of AI’ Geoffrey Hinton among Nobel Prize winners in physics
item.description: One of the most influential academics in artificial intelligence, Geoffrey E. Hinton, has been awarded the Nobel Prize in physics for training artificial neural networks and laying the foundations for today’s machine learning applications.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Lender seizes big San Jose office building in grim sign of market woes
item.description: Commercial real estate's slumping values are poised to become a widening challenge for Bay Area city, county, and regional agencies.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Customer Experience Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2024
item.description: Improving customer experience (CX) is a multi-faceted challenge to tackle as needs vary between stakeholders.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk, Tucker Carlson laugh about post discussing hypothetical Harris assassination
item.description: Conservative media pundit Tucker Carlson hosted tech mogul Elon Musk for an interview on his online video show this week, a sit-down that began with the two laughing about Musk's support for former President Trump and what he says was a "joke" he made about assassination attempts on political figure
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: The high-speed clay technique shaking up home construction
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how Clay’s impact printing technology enables fast, sustainable construction.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California’s vetoed AI bill: Bullet dodged, but not for long
item.description: Artificial intelligence has the power to revolutionize industries, drive economic growth, and improve our quality of life. But like any powerful, widely available technology, AI also poses significant risks.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: H-1B visa: Company supplying thousands of tech workers to Silicon Valley discriminated against non-Indians, jury finds
item.description: The H-1B has become a political flashpoint. Critics point to abuses including replacement of U.S. workers by visa holders, while the tech industry lobbies to boost the annual cap on new visas.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: The fact that it's a phone conversation — not just in-person chit-chat — could be part of the reason people find loud speakerphone calls so grating, speculated Ozlem Ayduk.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up challenge to Jack Smith’s Trump Twitter data access
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to take up a challenge by social platform X, formerly known as Twitter, to court rulings that forced the platform to turn over data on former President Trump’s account to special counsel Jack Smith.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Open source isn’t going to save AI
item.description: “You don’t want AI only in the hands of a few companies.” Thus spake Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue, suggesting that open source will come to the rescue of AI. It’s a nice thought, but one that has exactly zero historical precedent.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A flying shopping cart could make your future grocery runs that much easier
item.description: A flying shopping cart could revolutionize the way we shop and transport things. The Palletrone is a hovering platform that users push around at chest height.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments
item.description: Tim Gribben, the commissioner of the Bureau of the Fiscal Service, said the bureau refined and reframed its progress statement for the first time since 2018. The post Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news if a law passes forcing it to pay for content
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — Google said Friday it will stop linking to New Zealand news content and will reverse its support of local media outlets if the government passes a law forcing tech companies to pay for articles displayed on their platforms.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The creepy yet helpful humanoid robot ready to move into your home
item.description: A robot that stands more than 5 feet tall and weighs 66 pounds is well-equipped for household tasks, like carrying up to 44 pounds during its run time of two to four hours.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Starlink internet service being sent to places hit by Helene
item.description: FEMA said 60 Starlink units have been sent to multiple places devastated by the hurricane, with more to come.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation
